ENGINEER s
OFFICE IS


COSTLY

Deputies Gaily Take Long
Vacations on Full Pay


A Few Facts.

pages.raw_text:
While the probe Is being In.
serted in different departments at
the city hall It will not do to slight
the engineers office. That ancient
and tIme-honored institution OF
political and other activity can al.
ways be depended upon to produce
something worth going after, If the
inquisitor will but delve deep
enough.


The purpose of this article is not
to say anything harsh about the
engineers office at this time. be-
cause City Engineer Mccartney is
an awfully nice fellow, and The
Press hates like the mischief to
injure the feelings of good fell
lows. But there are one or two
things in connection with the Of
flee that it might not be amiss at
this time to call to the attention of
Mr.. Mccartney, just as they have
been placed before The Press.


To begin with, it is understood
that A. d. Butler, Macartney's s
chief deputy. has Just returned
from a 45-day vacation at full pay,
SITS per month. The city charter
says that 10 days is the extent of
the vacation allowed any one of.
ficial in single year-but what
does it matter, when we see Mayor
Pratt go away for five weeks and
pull down his little 1894 per
month on his return, just the same
T! Then there is Mr.. Klifeld. an
assistant engineer at $135 per. Mr.
klifeld wants to go to see the old
folks back in New York state OF
some other place in the east, SO
Mr.. Mccartney has asked the
board of public works or the coun.
Gil to send Mr.. Klifeld east at full
pay and expenses to attend a
paving convention of some sort. Ii
is to be hoped that the price of
paving will not be hoisted above
present figures on the return OF
Mr.. Klifeld. So it may be cheaper
for the taxpayers to pay the ex.
penses of Klifeld and let the paw.
ing business stand where it is, for
it is high enough already.


Then there Is the winter sched,
ule" in the engineers office, which
has just gone into effect. This
schedule works this way; All the
rod or men and inspectors EN-
ployed at SO per day are laid off
for the winter and the high priced
engineers are kept on. The engi
neers then turn In and act as rod
men for each other. in order tC
keep busy, the only difference be
ing that the city pays 81 or So pei
day for carrying the chain, while
formerly 1t paid but SO. The en-
gineers are mostly single fellows
who live well and need the money
while the rod men and inspectors
are largely common scrub tax
payers with families to support
and can i've during the winter on
what they earned in the summer
